---
title: "rieszboost"
subtitle: "Gradient boosting for Riesz representers"
---

`rieszboost` directly estimates the Riesz representer α₀ of a linear functional θ(P) = E[m(Z, g₀)] — the building block of one-step / TMLE / DML estimators in semiparametric inference — without ever deriving or inverting a propensity-style ratio.

It implements [Lee & Schuler, *RieszBoost* (arXiv:2501.04871)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.04871) with an sklearn-style API in Python and an R6 mirror in R. The R and Python interfaces produce **bitwise-identical** predictions; this guide presents every code example in both languages — pick whichever tab you prefer with the toggle on each page, and your choice persists site-wide.

## How to read this site

If you want to install the package and fit something in 30 lines, the [README](https://github.com/alejandroschuler/rieszboost) is the fastest path. This guide is the deeper reference:

- **[Why and what](intro.qmd)** — what's a Riesz representer, why estimate it directly.
- **[Install](install.qmd)** — Python install, optional R wrapper, dependencies.
- **[Quickstart](quickstart.qmd)** — end-to-end ATE in one page, executable, in both languages.
- **[Built-in and custom estimands](estimands.qmd)** — `ATE`, `ATT`, `TSM`, `AdditiveShift`, `LocalShift`, `StochasticIntervention`; how to write your own `m(z, alpha)`.
- **[scikit-learn integration](sklearn-integration.qmd)** — `cross_val_predict`, `GridSearchCV`, `Pipeline`, `clone`. (`RieszBooster` is a `BaseEstimator`.)
- **[Diagnostics and tuning](diagnostics-and-tuning.qmd)** — what `.diagnose()` tells you and how to tune in response.
- **[Backends and losses](backends-and-losses.qmd)** — swap to `SklearnBackend` for non-tree base learners; swap to `KLLoss` for density-ratio targets.
- **[R interface](r-interface.qmd)** — reticulate setup, parity guarantee, what's currently Python-only.
- **[Developing rieszboost](developing.qmd)** — repo layout, tests, how to contribute.
- **[References](references.qmd)** — papers cited throughout.

::: {.callout-tip title="Code execution"}
Every code block in this guide ran when the site was built. The numbers and figures you see came from the version of `rieszboost` that produced this page — there are no stale snippets.
:::
